print(f"\nDark gray squares: {[n['pixels'] for n in c_nodes]}")
</parameter>
</function>

Both green squares are at rows 33-35, and both dark gray squares are repaired. The level should be complete now, but it says "level_completed": false.

Maybe the goal is not just to repair the dark gray squares, but also to have the correct center colors. Let me think about what the correct center colors should be.

And the left dark gray square... it doesn't have an inner white area visible. The left green square has a W (white) center.
Wait, looking at the image, the left green square has a W (white) center, and the right green square has a c (charcoal) center. The right dark gray square has a white inner area. So maybe the right green square should have a W center to match, and the left green square should have a c center to match the left dark gray square.
But I can only swap center colors by clicking on a green square. If I click on the right green square, it becomes active and its center becomes W, while the left green square's center becomes c.
Let me try clicking on the right green square to swap the centers.
